rationale: The citing paper uses Wang et al. (2010) in its core theoretical setup to support a specific claim: choice is especially depleting when it involves difficult trade-offs. This is not just a bibliography-list mention; it helps justify the paper's premise that instrumental choice can consume mental resources. The citation also reappears in the general discussion as part of the key prior evidence base on depletion in choice. Even without direct method reuse, it meaningfully informs the paper's framing and positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Wang et al. (2010) as prior empirical support that trade-off-laden choices deplete executive resources, which underlies its argument about the costs of instrumental choice. It then builds its contribution by introducing experiential versus instrumental choice mode as a new distinction in this depletion literature.
